questions.question: Solve the inequality: 3x + 2 < 11
questions.explanation: Subtract 2 from both sides: 3x < 9. Divide both sides by 3: x < 3.

questions.question:
Which of the following values satisfies the inequality 2x − 5 ≥ 3? Write yes or no.
(a) x = 4
(b) x = 3

A phone plan costs $20 per month plus $0.15 per text. If your budget is at most $35, what is the maximum number of texts you can send?
Write your answer as an inequality and solve for the number of texts.
questions.explanation: Set up: 20 + 0.15t ≤ 35. Subtract 20: 0.15t ≤ 15. Divide by 0.15: t ≤ 100 texts.

questions.question: Solve: −2x > 8
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by −2. Remember: when dividing an inequality by a negative number, flip the inequality sign. So x < −4.

questions.question: Graph the inequality x ≥ −1 on a number line. Describe whether you use an open or closed circle and which direction the line goes.
questions.answer: Closed circle at −1, arrow pointing right
questions.explanation: The inequality x ≥ −1 includes −1 (≥ means 'greater than or equal to'), so use a closed circle. Values increase to the right.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: 1 < x + 2 ≤ 5
questions.explanation: Subtract 2 from all parts: 1 − 2 < x + 2 − 2 ≤ 5 − 2, which gives −1 < x ≤ 3.
